pairs.question: What is ThinkMode and what problem does it aim to solve?
pairs.answer: ThinkMode is a Chrome extension designed to help developers slow down and think more intentionally before and after using AI tools. It addresses the issue of developers accepting AI-generated answers without fully understanding the problem by introducing a 'thinking mode' selection and cognitive cost tracking system.

pairs.question: What are the five thinking modes recommended by ThinkMode and what purpose does each serve?
pairs.answer: ThinkMode recommends five thinking modes: Explore (understand the problem before solving it), Challenge (pressure-test an existing plan), Decide (compare options and tradeoffs), Audit (review quality, correctness, tests, and edge cases), and Reflect (learn from what you just did). Each mode is designed to match the prompt with the kind of thinking intended for the task.

pairs.question: How does ThinkMode calculate cognitive cost, and what happens when the cognitive cost meter is full?
pairs.answer: ThinkMode calculates cognitive cost by logging how AI is used, grouping usage into three categories: Supportive (AI expands your thinking), Mixed (AI saves time but may compress understanding), and Risky (AI may replace your judgment). Each log adds to the cognitive cost meter, and when it is full, ThinkMode temporarily pauses supported AI chat pages for 5 minutes.

pairs.question: What is the architecture of ThinkMode, and which technologies are used to build it?
pairs.answer: ThinkMode is a Manifest V3 Chrome extension built with React, TypeScript, and Vite. Its architecture includes a content script to detect AI chat pages, a floating button to open the extension, a background service worker to coordinate the side panel, a React side panel for the main workflow, and shared TypeScript modules for recommendation logic and prompt generation.

pairs.question: Does ThinkMode collect or store any user data? Provide a detailed explanation.
pairs.answer: No, ThinkMode does not read conversations, scrape page content, send analytics, or store data remotely. It operates entirely client-side with no backend or LLM API calls involved. The recommendation engine is deterministic and uses simple keyword rules to choose a thinking mode.

pairs.question: How can someone install ThinkMode, and what are the installation options?
pairs.answer: ThinkMode can be installed for free from the Chrome Web Store at [this link](https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/nbecnbmefilkkijpldhnbmmbcochhdpo). Alternatively, users can run the extension locally using Chrome's developer mode by downloading the open-source code from [GitHub](https://github.com/JulienAvezou/ai-thinking-mode-browser-extension) and forking it for customization.
